page: Microsoft Azure D48lds_v7 is a compute-optimized virtual machine from the Dldsv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 48 vCPUs mapped to 24 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor using the x86_64 architecture. The system is configured with 96.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. It includes 2835 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage, but does not include any GPU accelerators.

The underlying Intel Xeon processor includes a massive 480 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for tile and INT8 operations. In benchmark evaluations, the D48lds_v7 demonstrates strong single-core CPU performance and top-tier single-threaded compression and decompression capabilities, with gzip compression and bzip3 decompression placing in the top 10%. Multi-threaded decompression and Passmark CPU tests, including encryption and physics simulations, consistently rank in the top 25%.

While the 96.0 GB memory capacity is relatively low compared to the high vCPU count, the instance achieves strong cached memory bandwidth and strong performance in database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving. It also exhibits top-tier text generation speeds for small LLM inference workloads. This hardware profile offers a cost-efficient option for compute-heavy workloads that require high CPU performance and substantial local storage but do not demand massive memory footprints. Suitable workloads include web serving, database operations, compression tasks, and lightweight machine learning inference.
description: The Microsoft Azure D48lds_v7 is a compute-optimized virtual machine from the Dldsv7 family, powered by an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor on the x86_64 architecture. It features 48 vCPUs (24 physical cores), 96.0 GB of RAM, and 2835 GB of local storage, with no integrated GPU. This configuration provides a low-memory-to-CPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core, making it a cost-efficient choice for compute-intensive tasks that do not require massive memory footprints. Benchmarks show strong single-core performance, top-tier single-threaded compression, and strong database and Redis operations. The processor supports advanced vector and matrix extensions (AVX-512 and AMX) for accelerated mathematical operations. The D48lds_v7 is well-suited for web serving, database workloads, data compression, and small-scale language model inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D48lds_v7 is a compute-optimized VM featuring 48 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 96 GB RAM, and 2835 GB local storage, delivering strong single-core and database performance for compute-heavy workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D48lds_v7 is a compute-optimized VM with 48 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 96 GB RAM, and 2835 GB local storage for high-throughput workloads.
tagline: A compute-optimized virtual machine featuring forty-eight Intel Xeon vCPUs, high-speed local storage, and advanced matrix extensions for demanding workloads.
bullet_points: 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor
96.0 GB of system memory providing a 2.0 GB per vCPU ratio
2835 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated vector and matrix math
Top-tier performance in single-threaded compression and small LLM inference
Optimized for web serving, database operations, and compute-heavy tasks
